content.text:
## Auto.fun Updates

The Auto.fun platform is currently in maintenance mode while the team completes upgrades. Many users are eagerly awaiting its return, with some expressing frustration about the delay. The team has stated they want to ensure there are no more scaling issues before relaunching.

A team member confirmed: "we want to make sure we can release with no more scaling issues and don't have to pull the site again so being extra careful about the release. When it's ready, we'll give the heads up here and in the announcements channel before we do any kind of public marketing."

Some tokens created on April 22 cannot currently be queried or traded on the website. The team is aware and has added certain tokens to the import list for when the site returns.

Access to certain channels appears to be token-gated, with the "no access" channel reserved for holders of 100k+ tokens.

## Eliza Development Discussion

There were discussions about the upcoming v2 migration for Eliza, with users asking if it was supposed to happen this week. One user noted that the v2-develop branch doesn't have the agent.json file referenced in documentation.

A user reported issues with plugin development, questioning why it only supports local models rather than OpenAI integration. They shared error logs showing problems with the local model:

TypeError: text.match is not a function


Another user asked about URL-to-markdown tools, with recommendations for "markitdown" and "defuddle" (https://github.com/kepano/defuddle), with defuddle being highlighted as the best option for websites.

## Discord Summarization Pipeline

User dankvr (jin) shared that they had "refactored the discord summarization pipeline today, really dialed it in, nailed it" and are "summarizing multiple eliza / partner discords daily, results look fantastic." They shared a sample of the output format, noting it was "perfectly structured for LLMs to ingest."

Dankvr mentioned the next steps would be "RAG / AI agent integrations" and that "jintern boutta level up fr." They also republished a related post to Mirror: https://mirror.xyz/m3org.eth/Cg79LR5HTIomTs_O_YStj6sw6lkhCxop3vTyovnOSKQ

Another user expressed interest in deploying the tool to their AWS box.

## Discord Verification Issues

Several users reported problems with Discord verification and channel access. Some users lost their verified tags and couldn't see certain channels. A moderator manually assigned verified tags to help resolve these issues, though at least one user reported they could type in channels but still couldn't see content.

Users were advised to try verifying again, with one user suggesting they might need to connect a different wallet than the one they used when joining.

## Token-Gated Access Discussion

There was a discussion about token-gated access systems, with one user noting that AI16z was among the first to implement token-gated access for partners. Another user pointed out this wasn't a new concept, referencing "Crypto Kingdom" from 11 years ago that required 10,000 BTC for top-tier access ("supernode" clique), with lesser amounts granting other noble titles.

Users discussed how proper token-gating of partner groups could drive genuine demand for tokens, but noted they would need to "step our game up on benefits and privileges of being a partner." One user jokingly suggested a "Premium Prudent Spartan Lounge," while another noted that the only tangible benefit over the past 6 months was "the gold badge" and a Hyperfy airdrop.

categories.title: Recent Pull Requests in elizaOS/eliza Repository

content.text:
Several pull requests have been submitted to the elizaOS/eliza repository:

1. PR #4343 by ShahafGaimin adds a new AI Model Provider called Gaimin AI to the platform.

2. PR #4344 by wtfsayo implements a feature to skip displaying the banner if the terminal doesn't support UTF-8 encoding, improving compatibility across different environments.

3. PR #4337 by tcm390 fixes an issue where agents were not found after GUI creation and resolves merge conflicts in the codebase.

4. PR #4345 by yungalgo addresses a problem with the .gitignore file not being properly copied from the plugin-starter template.

categories.title: Discord Chat Analysis: ElizaOS Issues and System Upgrades

content.text:
## ElizaOS Technical Issues

Users are experiencing several technical difficulties with ElizaOS:

- Questions about the status of the v2 migration timeline
- Discrepancies between documentation and actual code functionality
- The `elizaos plugin` command only supports the `publish` subcommand despite documentation suggesting more functionality
- Local model implementation causing errors, including a specific TypeError related to text parsing
- Concerns about maintenance status of eliza-starter (last commit was 2 months ago)
- Dependency installation issues when using Docker containers

Users are also questioning why plugin development only uses local models instead of supporting OpenAI.

## System Upgrades Status

A team member (k_carv) has reported that system upgrades are nearly complete and updates will be provided soon. Multiple users have confirmed that the site is still down.

One specific technical issue was reported regarding tokens created on April 22 that cannot be queried or traded on the website. The user provided a hash (3hXD5KBcXiTxg7Hf1bBjnr83s34CpbEr976Ge8FEhzy9s666Jo8m8xLBsGzdykHMq7QiYewurtXWbrQH67xwWEuX) and contract address (5Vqjr3NkFrAxMzf6hmy8sYtZnhfreGi7w6TidmFQgFUN), but no solution was provided in the chat.

## Other Discussions

The Discord chat also included:

- Recommendations for URL-to-markdown tools (markitdown, defuddle, and firecrawl), with defuddle being recommended as the best option for websites that handle JavaScript/dynamic pages
- A verification issue where a user couldn't add channels with Pandez Guard Beta for verification
- Brief off-topic discussions about cryptocurrency projects

categories.title: Recent GitHub Issues in elizaOS/eliza Repository

content.text:
Two recent issues have been reported in the elizaOS/eliza GitHub repository:

1. Issue #4346: A user reported an error when attempting to send messages without an ANTHROPIC_API_KEY. The system returns an error message stating "x-api-key header is required". This suggests there may be an issue with how the application handles missing API keys or with the error messaging when the key is not provided.

2. Issue #4342: A user is experiencing failures when attempting to install the @elizaos/plugin-solana plugin. All installation attempts have failed, though the specific error details aren't provided in the issue title.

Current cryptocurrency prices:

- WETH (Wrapped Ethereum) is trading at $1,757.29
- SOL (Solana) is trading at $148.81

These prices reflect the current market values of these popular cryptocurrencies.

categories.title: Bugfix: Agent Not Found Error After GUI Creation

content.text: A bug was fixed that addressed an 'agent not found' error that occurred after GUI creation. The fix was implemented in pull request #4337, which also resolved a merge conflict.

The provided source only mentions 'Top contributors for elizaOS/eliza' without providing any specific details about who these contributors are or their contributions. Without additional information, it's not possible to generate a detailed summary of the top contributors to the elizaOS/eliza project.

## ElizaOS Development Challenges

Users are experiencing various technical issues with ElizaOS, particularly regarding the transition between different versions (main branch vs v2-develop). Key challenges include:

- Confusion about wallet integration for multi-user agents
- Problems with Twitter client functionality for handling replies
- Discrepancies between documentation and actual code implementation
- Plugin integration difficulties in v2
- Local model setup challenges
- UI functionality issues with image processing

The v2-develop branch appears to have significant structural changes compared to the main branch (0.x), causing compatibility issues with existing plugins. Users are struggling to understand how to properly implement custom plugins in v2 due to unclear documentation and changing folder structures. There's also confusion about model provider support, with some users noting that v2 seems to primarily support OpenAI and Anthropic while lacking support for other models.

## Auto.fun Website Updates

The auto.fun website is currently down for upgrades. Community members are eager for the release, especially given the current market conditions. A team member clarified that they're being cautious with the release to avoid having to take the site down again due to scaling problems:

"We want to make sure we can release with no more scaling issues and don't have to pull the site again so being extra careful about the release. When it's ready, we'll give the heads up here before we do any kind of public marketing."

There are also token visibility issues, with a team member confirming they've added specific tokens to an import list that will be visible once the site is back up. The team is selectively adding tokens based on liquidity requirements, declining to add test tokens with insufficient liquidity.

## Discord Summarization Pipeline

A user named "dankvr" mentioned refactoring a Discord summarization pipeline that creates structured summaries optimized for LLM ingestion. This appears to be a significant technical development, with the user sharing a sample of the output format and stating: "perfectly structured for LLMs to ingest."

The user also mentioned plans for RAG/AI agent integrations with the summarization pipeline, noting "now the fun part, RAG / AI agent integrations" and that "jintern boutta level up fr." Another user expressed interest in deploying the summarization tool to AWS.

## Token-Gated Access Discussions

Several users discussed the concept of token-gated partner access, noting historical precedents like "Crypto Kingdom" from 11 years ago that required 10,000 BTC for top-tier access. Users suggested that proper token-gating of partner groups could drive genuine demand for tokens, but emphasized the need to improve partner benefits and privileges.

One user suggested creating a "Premium Prudent Spartan Lounge" for partners, while another noted that the only tangible benefit over the past 6 months was "the gold badge (admittedly, a cool colour)" and a Hyperfy airdrop.

## Discord Verification Issues

Several users reported issues with Discord channel access and verification. A moderator manually assigned verified tags to restore access for affected users. One user reported being able to type in channels after receiving the tag but still couldn't see content.

The issue appears to be related to the verification system, with one moderator noting: "I think there's maybe something up with the verification -- had a few people flag they have been not able to see channels and they lost their tag."
